text:
After completing an install of 9.0-CURRENT on ZFS root
(http://wiki.freebsd.org/RootOnZFS/GPTZFSBoot), I receive this error
when trying to boot the computer after install:

error 1 lba 48
error 1 lba 1
No ZFS pools located, can't boot

Fix: 

Revert commits to zfsboot code around this time:

  http://svn.freebsd.org/viewvc/base/head/sys/boot/i386/zfsboot/zfsboot.c?r1=199714&r2=200309

Compile an older version of the boot code and write it to disk:

# cd /usr/src/sys/boot/
# make cleandir
# make cleandir
# make obj
# make depend
# make
# gpart bootcode -p /usr/obj/usr/src/sys/boot/i386/gptzfsboot/gptzfsboot -i 1 ad4

Keep a FreeBSD snapshot DVD handy to boot from :)
How-To-Repeat: Install FreeBSD using the following method on a Dell Adamo 13:

  http://wiki.freebsd.org/RootOnZFS/GPTZFSBoot

using a recent version of 8-STABLE or 9.0-CURRENT.
